title: Separable Verbs
description: Learn verbs that physically split apart! Start saying 'I pick you up' like a pro.
explanation:
**HOOK**
Imagine if the English verb 'pick up' physically split across the whole sentence, and the 'up' had to jump all the way to the very end. In German, this happens all the time! German verbs are like little transformers that break apart when they enter a sentence.

**CONCEPT**
We are learning verbs with 'prefixes' that split off. The most common ones are: anfangen (begin), anrufen (call), and aufstehen (stand up).

**RULE**
Verb King sits on Position 2 (the stem). The Prefix (the little word like 'auf' or 'an') gets catapulted all the way to the absolute end of the sentence.

tables.title: Common Splitters
tables.note: TIP: Only the stem part conjugates! 'Ich kaufe... ein', 'Du kaufst... ein'.

deep_notes.title: WATCH OUT
deep_notes.text:
When you use a Modal Verb (can, must, want), the separable verb DOES NOT split! It stays together at the end. 
Ich MUSS morgen AUFSTEHEN. (I must get up tomorrow).

examples.german: Ich stehe um sechs Uhr auf.
Wir kaufen heute im Supermarkt ein.
examples.english: We shop at the supermarket today.

exercises.question: Wann teilt sich ein trennbares Verb?
exercises.explanation: In a standard sentence, the prefix splits off and jumps to the end.

exercises.hint: Main stem of aufstehen (to get up).

exercises.question: Sag 'He calls her':
exercises.hint: Stem second, prefix at the end.
exercises.explanation: Er ruft (Stem) + sie (her) + an (Prefix at the end).

exercises.question: Was passiert mit 'einkaufen' mit einem Modalverb?
exercises.hint: Modal verbs KEEP the verb together at the end.
exercises.explanation: With a modal verb, they stay combined at the end!

exercises.question: Viele Leute sitzen jeden Abend auf der Couch und gucken TV. Sie sehen ____.
exercises.hint: TV-prefix. Drops to the end of the sentence.

exercises.instruction: Identify which separable verb was used:
Where is the prefix in the sentence you hear?
exercises.explanation: The prefix (an) jumps to the very end!

exercises.instruction: Identify the main verb (stem) you hear in Position 2:
exercises.audio_text: Er kauft heute im Supermarkt ein.
Ich stehe um sechs Uhr auf.
